title: Add M3 food and eating logistics slice
summary: Implement hunger-driven eating jobs using existing logistics, item, storage, reservation and pathing foundations.
acceptance: Hungry actors select edible resources through bounded indexed candidates and exact pathing only for Top-K entries
Eating jobs reserve source item quantity interaction spots and actor/job state atomically before consumption
Food quantity conservation holds across storage carried and consumed lanes with no negative resources or duplicate consumption
Failures emit structured reasons for no food permission path reservation ability schedule and stale owner state
Focused tests cover deterministic selection consumption cancellation and conservation

history.details: Created by WM-0045 M3 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.

objective: Connect M3 hunger to existing logistics without broad economy or content production.
forbiddenPaths: broad economy production chains or content catalog expansion
benchmarkImpact: Records edible candidate counts pathing counts reservation transactions conservation and hunger deltas; baseline updates wait for WM-0059.
reviewRouting: Simulation-engineer completes to reviewer; qa-performance can verify conservation and candidate cap evidence.
rollbackModel: Revert food/eating code tests docs and report; existing M2 logistics behavior remains intact.
ownerGate: Do not add broad economy or catalog production
Keep item quantities owned by item/storage stores
Use explicit serializable job state and reservation cleanup
Block on save protocol schema dependency UI authority or M4 scope

sparkEligibility.reason: Deterministic logistics, reservations and quantity ownership are outside Spark scope.
